node.natural_language_proof:
Proof: consider the set
`X = {(x1,...,xp) in G^p | x1 * x2 * ... * xp = 1}`. We can choose `x1,...,x_{p-1}` freely and then
`xp` is forced, so `|X| = |G|^(p-1)`, hence `p` divides `|X|` since `p` divides `|G|`.

Let the cyclic group of order `p` act on `X` by cyclic rotation of the coordinates. Each orbit has
size `1` or `p` (because `p` is prime). The fixed points are exactly the tuples of the form
`(x,...,x)`, and these are precisely the elements `x` with `x^p = 1`. Therefore `|X|` is congruent
mod `p` to the number of fixed points, i.e. to `|{x : G | x^p = 1}|`. Since `p` divides `|X|`, it
also divides the number of solutions to `x^p = 1`.

import Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Card
import Mathlib.Data.ZMod.Basic

variable {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G] [DecidableEq G]
variable {p : Nat} [Fact p.Prime]

theorem prime_dvd_card_solutions_pow_eq_one
    (hpdiv : p ∣ Fintype.card G) :
    p ∣ Fintype.card {x : G // x ^ p = 1} := by
  -- |vectorsProdEqOne G p| = |G|^(p-1)
  have hcard : Fintype.card (↑(Equiv.Perm.vectorsProdEqOne G p)) = Fintype.card G ^ (p - 1) := by
    apply Equiv.Perm.VectorsProdEqOne.card
  
  -- p divides |G|^(p-1) since p is prime and p divides |G|
  have hpdiv_card : p ∣ Fintype.card G ^ (p - 1) := by
    -- Use dvd_pow with (p - 1) ≠ 0
    have hp : p.Prime := Fact p.Prime.out
    exact hpdiv.pow (by
      -- p ≥ 2, so p - 1 ≥ 1 > 0
      have h : p ≥ 2 := hp.two_le
      have : (p - 1) ≥ 1 := by omega
      omega)
  
  -- Now relate vectorsProdEqOne G p to {x | x^p = 1}
  -- The fixed points of the rotation action on vectorsProdEqOne G p are exactly {(x, ..., x) | x^p = 1}
  -- By the orbit-counting lemma for p-groups: |X| ≡ |fixed points| (mod p)
  -- Since p divides |X|, p divides |{x | x^p = 1}|
